text:
In our application, on the Today page, if I go to the Today page, we have two screens: Execute and Plan. Once I go to the Plan page, there we see the timeline. If there is already at least one time block present, then it will show the timeline component here. In the timeline component, we have a highlight of the current time, kind of a single line that I can see now based on the current system time.
1. First of all, that line is good; I want that line, and it's looking good. The only problem is that, right now, in the design there is a point on the left, then there is a space, and then the line is starting, so there is a gap. I am not sure why.
2. Second, if, for example, at the current time we have a time block here and inside that time block there is some task assigned to that time block, what happens is that, due to this line, if that line is overlapping any of the tasks or anything, the text is not able to be clearly visible because it is getting hidden behind this timeline component, the current time component. We have to tweak and update the design for this time component.
3. Second thing is that, as soon as I load this Plan screen, by default my screen should scroll down so that my current time will always be in the center of my screen. As soon as I load this screen, I will directly see the timeline. Right now, if I load it, it will always start with 6 a.m., so I have to scroll down to see the current time and a time block and another task which is planned. To have a good understanding, as soon as I land on the screen, it should directly scroll so that the main highlight becomes the current time itself.
